title: Astracantha trojana
taxonomy: Astracantha trojana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is further classified as a member of the genus Astracantha and the subgenus Astragalus. The subgenus Astragalus is a subgenus of the genus Astracantha, which is a part of the legume family Fabaceae. The species Astracantha trojana is native to Turkey. It is a perennial plant that grows in rocky and mountainous areas.
uses: Astracantha trojana has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its roots and stems contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Astracantha trojana.

parts: Astracantha trojana. The medicinal parts of Astracantha trojana include roots, stems, and leaves. preparations: Astracantha trojana is not a commonly documented plant in Western herbalism, but traditional knowledge suggests that it has been used in folk medicine in the region where it is native, specifically in Turkey.
